aliases: How to configure tsconfig.json for TypeScript projects
tsconfig strict mode and moduleResolution setup

temporal_validity.last_breaking_change: TypeScript 5.5 (June 2024) — isolatedDeclarations, config extends array syntax

constraints: strict: true is non-negotiable for new projects — it enables strictNullChecks, noImplicitAny, and 7 other safety checks
moduleResolution must match your module setting — 'bundler' for Vite/webpack, 'nodenext' for pure Node.js
Never set skipLibCheck: false in large projects — it dramatically slows compilation
target and lib are separate concerns — target controls emit syntax, lib controls available type definitions
paths aliases require a corresponding setup in your bundler/runtime — TypeScript does NOT rewrite import paths at emit

skip_this_unit_if.condition: Using Deno (has built-in TypeScript with its own config)
Using Bun (bunfig.toml handles most TS config)

inputs_needed.question: Where does the compiled code run?
What type of project is this?
